Companies.name: Solar Panels GmbH
Companies.description: Leading manufacturer of high-efficiency photovoltaic solar panels and renewable energy solutions. Specializing in monocrystalline and polycrystalline solar cell technology with global distribution.

Business Capabilities.name: Research & Development
Business Capabilities.description: Innovation and development of solar technology solutions

Business Capabilities.name: Manufacturing Operations
Business Capabilities.description: Production of solar panels and related components

Business Capabilities.name: Supply Chain Management
Business Capabilities.description: Sourcing, procurement, and supplier relationship management

Business Capabilities.name: Sales & Marketing
Business Capabilities.description: Customer acquisition, relationship management, and market development

Business Capabilities.name: Customer Service & Support
Business Capabilities.description: Post-sales support, warranty management, and customer satisfaction

Business Capabilities.name: Quality Management
Business Capabilities.description: Quality assurance, testing, and compliance management

Business Capabilities.name: Finance & Accounting
Business Capabilities.description: Financial management, accounting, and reporting

Business Capabilities.name: Human Resources Management
Business Capabilities.description: Talent acquisition, development, and workforce management

Business Capabilities.name: IT Management
Business Capabilities.description: Information technology infrastructure and application management

Business Capabilities.name: Product Innovation
Business Capabilities.description: Development of new solar panel technologies and efficiency improvements

Business Capabilities.name: Materials Research
Business Capabilities.description: Research and development of new materials for solar cells

Business Capabilities.name: Prototype Development & Testing
Business Capabilities.description: Building and testing prototypes of new solar panel designs

Business Capabilities.name: Solar Cell Production
Business Capabilities.description: Manufacturing of photovoltaic cells

Business Capabilities.name: Module Assembly
Business Capabilities.description: Assembly of solar cells into complete solar modules

Business Capabilities.name: Packaging & Shipping
Business Capabilities.description: Packaging finished products and shipping to customers

Business Capabilities.name: Supplier Management
Business Capabilities.description: Management of supplier relationships and performance

Business Capabilities.name: Procurement
Business Capabilities.description: Sourcing and purchasing of raw materials and components

Business Capabilities.name: Inventory Management
Business Capabilities.description: Management of raw materials, work-in-progress, and finished goods inventory

Business Capabilities.name: Lead Generation
Business Capabilities.description: Generation of sales leads through various marketing channels

Business Capabilities.name: Sales Execution
Business Capabilities.description: Sales process execution and order management

Business Capabilities.name: Channel Management
Business Capabilities.description: Management of distribution channels and partner relationships

Business Capabilities.name: Incoming Material Inspection
Business Capabilities.description: Quality control of incoming raw materials and components

Business Capabilities.name: Production Testing
Business Capabilities.description: Testing of solar panels during and after production

Business Capabilities.name: Certification & Compliance
Business Capabilities.description: Ensuring products meet industry standards and certifications

Business Capabilities.name: Financial Planning & Analysis
Business Capabilities.description: Budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis

Business Capabilities.name: Accounts Payable
Business Capabilities.description: Supplier invoice processing and payment management

Business Capabilities.name: Financial Reporting
Business Capabilities.description: Generation of financial statements and regulatory reports

Business Capabilities.name: Talent Acquisition
Business Capabilities.description: Recruitment, hiring, and onboarding of employees

Business Capabilities.name: Performance Management
Business Capabilities.description: Employee performance evaluation and development

Business Capabilities.name: Payroll & Benefits Administration
Business Capabilities.description: Employee compensation and benefits management

Business Capabilities.name: IT Service Management
Business Capabilities.description: IT service desk, incident, and change management

Business Capabilities.name: Infrastructure Management
Business Capabilities.description: Management of IT infrastructure, servers, and networks

Business Capabilities.name: Application Management
Business Capabilities.description: Enterprise application lifecycle and portfolio management

Business Capabilities.name: Technical Support
Business Capabilities.description: Technical assistance and troubleshooting for customers

Business Capabilities.name: Warranty Management
Business Capabilities.description: Processing and management of product warranties and claims

Business Capabilities.name: Customer Training
Business Capabilities.description: Training and education services for customers

Architecture Principles.name: Business Agility First
Architecture Principles.description: Architecture must enable rapid business adaptation to market changes and customer demands
Architecture Principles.rationale: Solar industry is rapidly evolving with changing technologies and regulations
Architecture Principles.implications: Choose flexible platforms, avoid vendor lock-in, design for configurability

Architecture Principles.name: Customer-Centric Design
Architecture Principles.description: All systems and processes must prioritize customer experience and satisfaction
Architecture Principles.rationale: Customer satisfaction drives retention and growth in competitive solar market
Architecture Principles.implications: Design user-friendly interfaces, provide real-time information, enable self-service

Architecture Principles.name: Sustainability by Design
Architecture Principles.description: Technology choices must minimize environmental impact and support sustainability goals
Architecture Principles.rationale: As a solar company, environmental responsibility is core to our mission
Architecture Principles.implications: Choose energy-efficient technologies, optimize resource usage, measure carbon footprint

Architecture Principles.name: Data as Strategic Asset
Architecture Principles.description: Data must be treated as a valuable business asset with proper governance and monetization
Architecture Principles.rationale: Data-driven insights are crucial for R&D innovation and operational excellence
Architecture Principles.implications: Implement data governance, ensure data quality, create analytics capabilities

Architecture Principles.name: Single Source of Truth
Architecture Principles.description: Each data element should have one authoritative source across the enterprise
Architecture Principles.rationale: Consistent data enables reliable decision-making and reduces conflicts
Architecture Principles.implications: Define data ownership, implement master data management, eliminate data silos

Architecture Principles.name: Real-Time Operational Visibility
Architecture Principles.description: Critical business processes must provide real-time visibility and monitoring
Architecture Principles.rationale: Manufacturing efficiency and quality control require immediate feedback and response
Architecture Principles.implications: Implement IoT sensors, streaming analytics, and real-time dashboards

Architecture Principles.name: Cloud-First Strategy
Architecture Principles.description: New applications should be cloud-native, existing applications migrated to cloud when feasible
Architecture Principles.rationale: Cloud provides scalability, cost efficiency, and access to latest technologies
Architecture Principles.implications: Design for cloud-native patterns, use managed services, plan migration roadmap

Architecture Principles.name: API-First Design
Architecture Principles.description: All applications must expose well-designed APIs for integration and ecosystem development
Architecture Principles.rationale: APIs enable integration, partner ecosystem, and future innovation
Architecture Principles.implications: Design APIs before implementation, follow REST/GraphQL standards, provide documentation

Architecture Principles.name: Microservices for Complex Domains
Architecture Principles.description: Complex business domains should be implemented using microservices architecture
Architecture Principles.rationale: Microservices enable independent development, scaling, and deployment
Architecture Principles.implications: Define service boundaries, implement service mesh, plan for distributed complexity

Architecture Principles.name: Automation-First Approach
Architecture Principles.description: Manual processes should be automated wherever technically and economically feasible
Architecture Principles.rationale: Automation reduces errors, improves efficiency, and enables scaling
Architecture Principles.implications: Implement CI/CD, infrastructure as code, automated testing, and monitoring

Architecture Principles.name: Prefer Open Standards
Architecture Principles.description: Choose open standards and avoid proprietary technologies when possible
Architecture Principles.rationale: Open standards reduce vendor lock-in and improve interoperability
Architecture Principles.implications: Evaluate open-source alternatives, avoid proprietary formats, plan migration strategies

Architecture Principles.name: Edge Computing for Manufacturing
Architecture Principles.description: Processing should occur close to manufacturing equipment for real-time control
Architecture Principles.rationale: Manufacturing requires low-latency processing for quality control and safety
Architecture Principles.implications: Deploy edge computing infrastructure, hybrid cloud architecture, local processing capabilities

Architecture Principles.name: Zero Trust Security Model
Architecture Principles.description: Never trust, always verify - implement comprehensive identity and access management
Architecture Principles.rationale: Cyber threats require robust security measures to protect IP and operations
Architecture Principles.implications: Implement identity management, network segmentation, continuous monitoring

Architecture Principles.name: Data Protection by Design
Architecture Principles.description: Data protection and privacy must be built into systems from the ground up
Architecture Principles.rationale: Regulatory compliance and customer trust require strong data protection
Architecture Principles.implications: Implement encryption, access controls, audit trails, and privacy controls

Architecture Principles.name: Event-Driven Architecture
Architecture Principles.description: Use event-driven patterns for loose coupling and real-time data processing
Architecture Principles.rationale: Manufacturing and supply chain require real-time event processing
Architecture Principles.implications: Implement event streaming, message queues, and event sourcing patterns

Architecture Principles.name: Asynchronous Communication
Architecture Principles.description: Prefer asynchronous communication patterns for better resilience and scalability
Architecture Principles.rationale: Asynchronous patterns improve system resilience and user experience
Architecture Principles.implications: Use message queues, async APIs, and eventual consistency patterns

Architecture Principles.name: Performance by Design
Architecture Principles.description: Performance requirements must be considered from the initial design phase
Architecture Principles.rationale: Manufacturing systems require consistent high performance for operational efficiency
Architecture Principles.implications: Define performance requirements, implement monitoring, optimize critical paths

Architecture Principles.name: Technology Cost Transparency
Architecture Principles.description: Technology costs must be visible and allocated to business units for accountability
Architecture Principles.rationale: Cost visibility enables better resource allocation and optimization decisions
Architecture Principles.implications: Implement cost tracking, chargeback models, and resource optimization

Infrastructure.name: AWS US East 1 (Virginia)
Infrastructure.description: Primary AWS region for production workloads
Infrastructure.specifications: AWS Region with multiple Availability Zones

Infrastructure.name: AWS EU West 1 (Ireland)
Infrastructure.description: Secondary AWS region for disaster recovery and European data residency

Infrastructure.name: EKS Production Cluster
Infrastructure.description: Amazon EKS cluster for production containerized applications
Infrastructure.specifications: m5.large to m5.4xlarge instances, managed node groups

Infrastructure.name: EKS Development Cluster
Infrastructure.description: Amazon EKS cluster for development and testing
Infrastructure.specifications: t3.medium to m5.large instances, spot instances enabled

Infrastructure.name: ECS Fargate Cluster
Infrastructure.description: Amazon ECS cluster using Fargate for serverless containers

Infrastructure.name: SAP S/4HANA VM Cluster
Infrastructure.description: High-performance virtual machines for SAP S/4HANA
Infrastructure.specifications: 16 vCPU, 128 GB RAM, 1000 GB SSD each

Infrastructure.name: Oracle SCM VM Cluster
Infrastructure.description: Virtual machines for Oracle Supply Chain Management
Infrastructure.specifications: 8 vCPU, 64 GB RAM, 500 GB SSD each

Infrastructure.name: Manufacturing Datacenter
Infrastructure.description: On-premise datacenter at manufacturing facility for local systems
Infrastructure.location: Solar Panel Manufacturing Facility, Phoenix, AZ

Infrastructure.name: Siemens PLM Servers
Infrastructure.description: Physical servers for Siemens Teamcenter PLM system
Infrastructure.specifications: 2x Intel Xeon Silver 4314, 128 GB RAM, 2TB SSD RAID

Infrastructure.name: Manhattan WMS Servers
Infrastructure.description: Physical servers for warehouse management system
Infrastructure.specifications: 2x Intel Xeon Gold 5218, 64 GB RAM, 1TB SSD RAID

Infrastructure.name: EKS Europe Cluster
Infrastructure.description: Planned Amazon EKS cluster in EU region for data residency compliance
Infrastructure.specifications: m5.large to m5.2xlarge instances, planned for Q2 2025

AI Components.name: Solar Panel Quality Prediction System
AI Components.description: Machine learning model predicting solar panel defects and quality issues during production

AI Components.name: Automated Visual Defect Detection
AI Components.description: Computer vision system for automated detection of surface defects and anomalies in solar panels

AI Components.name: Equipment Maintenance Predictor
AI Components.description: Predictive analytics system forecasting equipment maintenance needs and potential failures

AI Components.name: Solar Market Demand Forecaster
AI Components.description: Advanced analytics system predicting solar panel demand based on market trends and weather patterns

AI Components.name: SolarBot Customer Assistant
AI Components.description: AI-powered chatbot providing 24/7 customer support and technical assistance for solar panel customers

AI Components.name: Supply Chain Optimization Engine
AI Components.description: AI system optimizing supply chain logistics, inventory levels, and supplier selection

AI Components.name: Solar Energy Production Forecaster
AI Components.description: Deep learning model predicting solar energy output based on weather data and panel performance

AI Components.name: Solar Solution Recommendation Engine
AI Components.description: AI system recommending optimal solar panel configurations based on customer requirements and conditions

AI Components.name: Dynamic Pricing Optimization System
AI Components.description: AI-driven pricing engine optimizing solar panel prices based on market conditions and competition

AI Components.name: Carbon Footprint Analytics System
AI Components.description: AI system analyzing and optimizing the environmental impact and sustainability metrics of operations

Visions.name: Solar Market Leadership 2030
Visions.visionStatement: By 2030 Solar Panels GmbH is the most trusted European provider of intelligent, sustainable photovoltaic solutions.

Missions.name: Deliver Reliable Clean Energy
Missions.purposeStatement: Deliver high-performance solar modules with predictable quality and lifecycle reliability.

Missions.name: Scale Efficient Solar Production
Missions.purposeStatement: Scale global manufacturing throughput while reducing conversion cost and process waste.

Values.name: Sustainability First
Values.valueStatement: We make long-term environmental impact the baseline for every strategic and operational decision.

Values.name: Customer Obsession
Values.valueStatement: We design processes, products, and services starting from customer outcomes.

Values.name: Quality with Integrity
Values.valueStatement: We maintain uncompromising quality standards and transparent accountability in all deliveries.

Goals.name: Expand DACH Market Share
Goals.goalStatement: Increase DACH market share from 12% to 18% by 2028 through channel and product expansion.

Goals.name: Reduce Production Cost per Watt
Goals.goalStatement: Reduce production cost per watt by 20% by 2027 through automation and yield optimization.

Goals.name: Improve Customer NPS
Goals.goalStatement: Increase customer net promoter score from 42 to 60 by 2027 via proactive service and transparency.

Strategies.name: Smart Factory Rollout
Strategies.description: Introduce MES-led digital production control, automated quality checkpoints, and machine telemetry integration.

Strategies.name: Growth Through Channels
Strategies.description: Scale channel partner enablement and targeted campaigns to increase market presence in strategic regions.

Strategies.name: Digital Customer Journey
Strategies.description: Unify CRM, marketing, and service touchpoints to provide transparent, personalized customer interactions.
